subject: [PATCH v2] fuse: fix io-uring background queue dispatch on request completion

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: reviewer questioned whether the patch guarantees progress when there are still requests on fc->bg_queue at ring readiness, and suggested adding a comment to clarify this subtlety
reviews.raw_body:
Does this guarantee progress if there are still requests on
fc->bg_queue at the point when ring becomes ready?

Seems so, because there must be at least one background request on the
regular request queues if bg_queue is non-empty, and when that is
finished, a new one will be put on the pending queue, and so on until
bg_queue becomes empty.

Maybe add a comment about this subtlety?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Bernd Schubert noted that request allocation is still blocked until the ring is set to ready, and suggested that there should be no background requests at all; he also mentioned a lock order change issue in dynamic switching from /dev/fuse to fuse-io-uring

The reviewer noted that io-uring background queues are designed to always allow at least one pending request, regardless of the global background limit, in order to prevent stalls.

Reviewer Bernd Schubert asked where the requested comment about accounting connection-level background counters before flushing the queue's background queue should be placed.
reviews.raw_body:
Request allocation is still blocked until the ring is set to ready -
there should be no background requests at all.
There were a couple of issues with dynamic switching from /dev/fuse to
fuse-io-uring, a really hard one was a lock order change. I think once
we have reduced queues merged, I'm to create an additional patch set
which allow allow distribution of requests between queues. The initial
series had that, but Joanne had concerns. Anyway, once we have
distribution between queues, we can avoid the global bg lock and global
bg limit and set that per queue. If I remember right, that would solve
the lock order issue. Although I forgot the exact details. Wish I would
have creates notes or a blog - too late now :(

---

Yeah, in order to avoid stalls, ring queues always allow one bg request,
ignoring the global limit.

---

Where should that comment be?


Thanks,
Bernd

2026-04-16.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation where background requests completed via io-uring were not properly dispatched due to incorrect handling of connection-level background counters. The fix involves accounting for these counters before flushing the queue's background queue, ensuring that pending background requests are dispatched correctly. This patch addresses a problem introduced by a previous commit that allowed bg requests to be queued through io-uring, and it has been reviewed and tested to ensure correctness.
